perturbation.original_text: 1.19 "Required Standards" means Applicable Law, the Specifications, and the warranties given by Sutro in Section 7.3, provided that Required Standards shall not include compliance with cGMPs for Custom Reagents, Extract identified as "research grade" Extract or Other Extracts.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.19 "Required Standards" means Applicable Law, the Specifications, and the warranties given by Sutro in Section 7.3, provided that Required Standards *shall* include compliance with cGMPs for Custom Reagents, Extract identified as "research grade" Extract or Other Extracts.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not include' to 'shall include', the definition of 'Required Standards' now explicitly includes compliance with cGMPs for certain products where it previously did not. This contradicts Section 2.8, which implies that cGMP should be applied when the Required Standards are applied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sutro shall Manufacture the Product(s) in accordance with the Required Standards, as the same may be amended or supplemented from time to time. Each Party shall keep the other promptly and fully advised of any new requirements of the applicable Regulatory Authority or Applicable Law of which it becomes aware and Sutro shall promptly implement such requirements as described in Section 6.6.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes compliance with cGMPs for certain products in the definition of 'Required Standards'. The changed text explicitly includes them. Section 2.8 requires Sutro to manufacture products in accordance with 'Required Standards'. Therefore, the change directly contradicts the original agreement by mandating cGMP compliance where it was previously excluded, creating a direct conflict and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 2.6 Delivery and Risk of Loss. Sutro shall mark Product for delivery to the destination(s) specified by SutroVax. All shipments of Product(s) shall be delivered [***] (lncoterms 2010) Sutro Facility. Title and risk of loss and damage to the Product(s) shall remain with Sutro until the Product(s) are delivered in accordance with the foregoing, at which time title and risk ofloss and damage to the Product(s) shall pass to SutroVax. SutroVax will arrange for shipping from Sutro's Facility to the destination specified by SutroVax at SutroVax's cost and expense.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.6 Delivery and Risk of Loss. Sutro shall mark Product for delivery to the destination(s) specified by SutroVax. All shipments of Product(s) shall be delivered [***] (lncoterms 2010) Sutro Facility. Title and risk of loss and damage to the Product(s) shall pass to SutroVax upon shipment from Sutro's facility, irrespective of whether it reaches SutroVax. SutroVax will arrange for shipping from Sutro's Facility to the destination specified by SutroVax at SutroVax's cost and expense.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that title and risk of loss pass to SutroVax *when the products are delivered*. The modified text states that title and risk of loss pass *upon shipment*, regardless of whether the goods are received. This creates a contradiction because the initial clause implies a successful delivery is required for transfer of risk, while the modification removes this requirement and shifts the risk even if the product is lost or damaged in transit. This contradiction is being made against the original text within the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Delivery and Risk of Loss. Sutro shall mark Product for delivery to the destination(s) specified by SutroVax. All shipments of Product(s) shall be delivered [***] (lncoterms 2010) Sutro Facility. Title and risk of loss and damage to the Product(s) shall pass to SutroVax upon shipment from Sutro's facility, irrespective of whether it reaches SutroVax. SutroVax will arrange for shipping from Sutro's Facility to the destination specified by SutroVax at SutroVax's cost and expense.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction within section 2.6. The original text states that title and risk of loss pass to SutroVax when the products are delivered. The modified text states that title and risk of loss pass upon shipment, irrespective of whether it reaches SutroVax. This directly conflicts with the initial condition of delivery being required, thereby creating an ambiguity regarding when SutroVax assumes responsibility for the products. This qualifies as an 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the key obligation concerning the transfer of risk is defined inconsistently within the section, making enforcement difficult and the parties' responsibilities unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Right to Audit. During the Term and the [***] period thereafter, SutroVax or a SutroVax Affiliate may, during normal working hours and upon reasonable advance notice perform site audits and inspect, or request information relating to, Sutro's or its subcontractor's Facilities and records directly or indirectly involved in the performance of this Supply Agreement or related to the Product(s). Such requests should be made in writing and Sutro will allow for such audits or inspection to occur within [***] days from request (excepting for cause audits) for Sutro's Facilities and within [***] days' from request (excepting for cause audits) for Sutro's subcontractor's facilities. Reasonable advance notice for audits for cause shall not require more than [***] advance notice. During such an inspection or request for information the inspectors may inquire about the progress of the work being carried out by Sutro or its subcontractor, and are in particular but not exclusively authorized to:
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Right to Audit. During the Term and the [***] period thereafter, SutroVax or a SutroVax Affiliate *must*, during normal working hours and upon reasonable advance notice perform site audits and inspect, or request information relating to, Sutro's or its subcontractor's Facilities and records directly or indirectly involved in the performance of this Supply Agreement or related to the Product(s). Such requests should be made in writing and Sutro will allow for such audits or inspection to occur within [***] days from request (excepting for cause audits) for Sutro's Facilities and within [***] days' from request (excepting for cause audits) for Sutro's subcontractor's facilities. Reasonable advance notice for audits for cause shall not require more than [***] advance notice. During such an inspection or request for information the inspectors may inquire about the progress of the work being carried out by Sutro or its subcontractor, and are in particular but not exclusively authorized to:
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that SutroVax *may* perform site audits, implying it's optional. The modified text changes this to SutroVax *must* perform site audits, making it mandatory. This creates a contradiction with the phrase 'Right to Audit' within the name of the section, which insinuates a right but not an obligation. Sutro now also, in the context of potential future issues, now can raise this clause to show reason for breach. Also with respect to the text in the section. This contradiction is within the section itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Right to Audit. During the Term and the [***] period thereafter, SutroVax or a SutroVax Affiliate must, during normal working hours and upon reasonable advance notice perform site audits and inspect, or request information relating to, Sutro's or its subcontractor's Facilities and records directly or indirectly involved in the performance of this Supply Agreement or related to the Product(s). Such requests should be made in writing and Sutro will allow for such audits or inspection to occur within [***] days from request (excepting for cause audits) for Sutro's Facilities and within [***] days' from request (excepting for cause audits) for Sutro's subcontractor's facilities. Reasonable advance notice for audits for cause shall not require more than [***] advance notice. During such an inspection or request for information the inspectors may inquire about the progress of the work being carried out by Sutro or its subcontractor, and are in particular but not exclusively authorized to:
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'may' indicating an optional action for SutroVax to perform site audits, while the changed text uses 'must,' creating a mandatory obligation. This directly contradicts the implication within the section title 'Right to Audit,' which suggests a right, not an obligation. The contradictory use of 'may' and 'must' creates a clear conflict within the same section, leading to potential disputes. The changed text therefore causes ambiguity and contradiction within the section, as to what is legally required of SutroVax, or not.
